feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Energy constraint on human health
entry.content:
Authors: A Behnke, E Shaulson, H Pontzer, CPKempes... - Trends in Endocrinology & ..., 2026

Abstract: Evolved constraints to human energy transformation force the body–brain system to operate an economy of energy. To survive and thrive, an organism's finite internal energy resources must be dynamically reallocated, forcing trade-offs from organelle ...

Tags: brain

entry.title: Programmable Coacervates Based on Minimalist Sticker–Spacer Frameworks: Chemical Design, Functions, and Emerging Applications

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: N Essa, M Abbas - ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces, 2026

Abstract: ... (11−13) Similarly, amphiphilic polymersomes have been extensively explored as protocell models due to their ability to self-assemble into ... Owing to their life-like properties, coacervates are widely regarded as protocell models with the potential to ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Origin of Life

entry.title: Bitter almond gum as a nutraceutical intervention for lipid profile and anthropometry in women with T2DM: triple-blinded, randomized, placebo-controlled study

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: Z Rafiei, P Dehghan, S Aliasgharzadeh, NL Yagin... - Journal of Functional Foods, 2026

Abstract: Bitter almond (Amygdalus communis L. var. amara) gum—a soluble fiber-rich exudate—has been suggested to exhibit prebiotic properties and confer potential cardiometabolic benefits. This study evaluated the effects of bitter almond gum on ...

entry.title: Physicochemical characterization and in vitro digestibility of pretreated green banana flour through hot air oven drying

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: A Mushtaq, M Hussain, SH Phyo, MU Khalid, L Li... - Applied Food Research, 2026

Abstract: The potential functionality and health benefits of green banana flour (GBF) arise from its resistant starch (RS) and high bioactive compound concentrations. Thus, we investigated the physicochemical, structural, in vitro digestibility, starch hydrolysis ...

entry.title: Mesoporous silica nanoparticles encapsulating xylanase: Efficient platform for low-polymerization-degree xylooligosaccharides preparation

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: X Zhu, A Li, C Huang, Y Du, Q Yong - Bioresource Technology, 2026

Abstract: Xylooligosaccharides (XOS) are prebiotic oligosaccharides that can be efficiently produced through enzymatic hydrolysis. Therefore, the oriented control of the degree of polymerization (DP) of oligosaccharides obtained through enzymatic ...

entry.title: Prebiotic -based" Shield" hydrogel embedded with rod-shaped Mn 2 (Olsa) MOF particles for inflammatory bowel disease and associated mental disorders

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: X Wang, W Su, X Li, H Xu, H Tian, L Huang, L Guo... - Nano Research, 2026

Abstract: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), with its colonic predilection, was frequently complicated by neuropsychiatric comorbidities such as anxiety and depression. To address this, we developed a colon-targeted, prebiotic -based synergistic therapeutic ...

Abstract: ... origin of life , yet the molecular nature of the earliest abiotic membranes remains uncertain. Existing models based on simple fatty acids, while experimentally tractable, often lack the environmental robustness required under fluctuating ...

entry.title: Emergence biases in molecular evolution
entry.content:
Authors: T Fuqua, N Vakirlis -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.20477, 2026

Abstract: ... , emergence bias, to provide support for this new concept , and speculate on its molecular underpinnings. We believe that emergence biases ... Here, we present evidence for emergence biases across different subfields of molecular biology, and ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: The emergence of (3+ 1)-dimensional expanding spacetime from complex Langevin simulations of the Lorentzian type IIB matrix model with deformations
entry.content:
Authors: KN Anagnostopoulos, T Azuma, M Hirasawa... -arXivpreprintarXiv..., 2026

Abstract: ... The near-horizontal distribution of ⟨αa⟩ at late times (large |αa|) indicates the emergence of real time. We find that one of the nine eigenvalues of Tij(t) grows with t. While this was misinterpreted as an evidence for the emergence of an expanding (1+1)-dimensional ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Complexity

entry.title: Autonomous Emergence of Hamiltonian in Deep Generative Models
entry.content:
Authors: W Xi, WQ Chen -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.20821, 2026

Abstract: The unprecedented predictive success of deep generative models in complex many-body systems, such as AlphaFold3, raises an epistemological question: do these networks merely memorize data distributions via high-dimensional interpolation, or ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Complexity

entry.title: Emergence of Transport Regimes from the Axial Field-Induced Interfacial Gradients in Uniform Surface Potential Nanopores
entry.content:
Authors: P Srinivasula, D Pandey -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.20379, 2026

Abstract: Gate-modulated nanopores have emerged as a promising platform for achieving ion selectivity and ionic current rectification (ICR) with the advantage of active field-based control. However, the mechanistic origin of these experimentally reported ...

entry.title: Emergence Transformer: Dynamical Temporal Attention Matters
entry.content:
Authors: Z Zhou, BW Qin, K Du, W Lin -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.19816, 2026

Abstract: ... in emergence phenomenon of complex systems including oscillatory coherence in quantum, biophysical, or climate systems. Here, by designing dynamical temporal attention (DTA) with timevarying query, key, and value matrices, we propose an ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: More Is Different: Toward a Theory of Emergence in AI-Native Software Ecosystems
entry.content:
Authors: D Russo -arXivpreprintarXiv:2604.19827, 2026

Abstract: Software engineering faces a fundamental challenge: multi-agent AI systems fail in ways that defy explanation by traditional theories. While individual agents perform correctly, their interactions degrade entire ecosystems, revealing a gap in our ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Complexity
